Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Maibara City|ja|米原市|12=draft}} Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=draft}} precincts) |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 47.98 |coordinates = 136|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 19.59 |omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=draft}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-{{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|12=draft}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|12=draft}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|12=draft}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Maibara City|ja|米原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}}. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=draft}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a stone coffin and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=draft}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the {{ill|Sakata District|ja|坂田郡|12=draft}} area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|12=draft}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|ja|1882nd year|12=draft}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering stone coffins and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|ja|1940th year|12=draft}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|ja|1957th year|12=draft}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969th year|12=draft}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year|12=draft}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|12=draft}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|12=draft}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|12=draft}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=draft}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. File:山津照神社古墳出土 装身具類.JPG|Copper mirrors and magatama beads
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 馬具類.JPG|Horse trappings
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Sue pottery pottery
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=draft}} - Houses and displays burial goods from the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. * Maibara City Okina Haniwa Museum - Features a reconstructed model of the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly cited in this article)}} * Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. "Exploring the 6th Century Around Lake Biwa." Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a stone coffin and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=draft|12=draft}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the {{ill|Sakata District|ja|坂田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|12=draft|12=draft}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|ja|1882nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering stone coffins and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|ja|1940th year|12=draft|12=draft}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|ja|1957th year|12=draft|12=draft}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969th year|12=draft|12=draft}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year|12=draft|12=draft}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|12=draft|12=draft}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|12=draft|12=draft}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Maibara City|ja|米原市|12=draft|12=draft}} Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=draft|12=draft}} precincts) |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 47.98 |coordinates = 136|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 19.59 |omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=draft|12=draft}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-{{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|12=draft|12=draft}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|12=draft|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Maibara City|ja|米原市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=draft|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a stone coffin and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=draft|12=draft}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the {{ill|Sakata District|ja|坂田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|12=draft|12=draft}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|ja|1882nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering stone coffins and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|ja|1940th year|12=draft|12=draft}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|ja|1957th year|12=draft|12=draft}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969th year|12=draft|12=draft}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year|12=draft|12=draft}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|12=draft|12=draft}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|12=draft|12=draft}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|12=draft|12=draft}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|ja|1882nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering stone coffins and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|ja|1940th year|12=draft|12=draft}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|ja|1957th year|12=draft|12=draft}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969th year|12=draft|12=draft}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year|12=draft|12=draft}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|12=draft|12=draft}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|12=draft|12=draft}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. "Exploring the 6th Century Around Lake Biwa." Kyoto University, 1995. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} * {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|12=draft|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Okina Clan burials]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:山津照神社古墳]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:山津照神社古墳]] [[ja:山湾照神社古壇]] [[en:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |image = [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Maibara City|ja|米原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} precincts) |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 47.98 |coordinates = 136|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 19.59 |omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-{{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Maibara City|ja|米原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a stone coffin and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the {{ill|Sakata District|ja|坂田郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|ja|1882nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering stone coffins and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|ja|1940th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|ja|1957th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. Kyoto University, 1995. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} * {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{citation needed|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Okina Clan burials]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:山津照神社古墳]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:山津照神社古墳]] [[ja:山湾照神社古壇]] [[en:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]]

revision.comment: 13 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:山津照神社古墳 up to rev 99995252
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 山津照神社古墳 |画像 = [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = 能登瀬古墳{{Sfn|山津照神社古墳（古墳）|1989}} |所属 = 息長古墳群 |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[米原市]]能登瀬598（字オホギ）（[[山津照神社]]境内） |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 19|緯度秒 = 47.98 |経度度 =136|経度分 = 19|経度秒 = 19.59 |ISO = JP-25 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長46.2m |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]]（内部に[[家形石棺]]） |被葬者 = （一説）[[息長氏]]首長 |出土品 = 副葬品多数・[[埴輪]]片・土器片 |史跡 = 岐阜県指定史跡「山津照神社古墳」 |有形文化財 = 出土品（滋賀県指定文化財） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Shiga |ラベル = 山津照神社
古墳 |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''山津照神社古墳'''（やまつてるじんじゃこふん）は、[[滋賀県]][[米原市]]能登瀬にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。息長古墳群（おきながこふんぐん）を構成する古墳の1つ。滋賀県指定史跡に指定され、出土品は滋賀県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|墳丘（北側）}}{{small|右に前方部、左奥に後円部。}}]] 滋賀県北東部、湖北地方の横山丘陵の南端部に築造された、湖北地方では代表的な後期古墳である{{Sfn|近江町山津照神社古墳の調査|1995}}。一帯では塚の越古墳、後別当古墳、人塚山古墳、狐塚古墳などとともに息長古墳群を構成する{{Sfn|山津照神社古墳（平凡社）|1991}}。現在は[[式内社]]の[[山津照神社]]境内に位置し、[[1882年]]（[[明治]]15年）の山津照神社での工事の際に発見され、石棺・遺物が出土している。現在までに墳丘の一部が削平を受けているほか、[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）には[[京都大学]]による調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方部が発達した後期の前方後円形で、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘長は46.2メートルを測る。墳丘表面では段築・葺石は認められていないが、石見型盾形埴輪・[[円筒埴輪]]が巡らされるほか、北側くびれ部では祭祀跡と見られる遺構から[[造出]]の存在可能性が指摘される{{Sfn|近江町山津照神社古墳の調査|1995}}。周溝は認められていない{{Sfn|近江町山津照神社古墳の調査|1995}}。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、内部に[[家形石棺]]が据えられている。この石棺からは、明治の発見の際に金銅製冠・刀剣・水晶製三輪玉など多数の副葬品が出土している。 この山津照神社古墳は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]中葉頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|近江町山津照神社古墳の調査|1995}}。息長古墳群では塚の越古墳に後続する首長墓に位置づけられるとともに{{Sfn|近江町山津照神社古墳の調査|1995}}、息長古墳群ひいては[[坂田郡]]一帯では最後の前方後円墳とされる{{Sfn|水谷千秋|2013|pp=76-97}}。息長古墳群は坂田郡の豪族の[[息長氏]]（おきながうじ）の首長墓群と推測されており、本古墳もその1つとされる{{Sfn|山津照神社古墳（平凡社）|1991}}{{Sfn|水谷千秋|2013|pp=76-97}}（古くは[[神功皇后]]の父の[[息長宿禰王]]の墓とする説もあった{{Sfn|山津照神社古墳（古墳）|1989}}）。琵琶湖対岸では同時期の古墳として[[稲荷山古墳 (高島市)|鴨稲荷山古墳]]（[[高島市]]鴨）も知られ、これらの古墳を営造した息長氏・三尾氏や両氏が関係する[[継体天皇]]を巡り、当時の歴史的背景を考察する説も挙げられている{{Sfn|水谷千秋|2013|pp=76-97}}。 古墳域は[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）に滋賀県指定史跡に指定されているほか、出土品は[[1957年]]（昭和32年）に滋賀県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1882年]]（[[明治]]15年）、山津照神社での工事の際に発見、石棺・遺物出土{{Sfn|山津照神社古墳（平凡社）|1991}}。 * [[1940年]]（[[昭和]]15年）、出土遺物調査（[[京都大学]]考古学教室）{{Sfn|近江町山津照神社古墳の調査|1995}}。 * [[1957年]]（昭和32年）8月26日、出土品の大部分が滋賀県指定有形文化財に指定。 * [[1969年]]（昭和44年）9月12日、滋賀県指定史跡に指定。 * [[1994年]]度（[[平成]]6年度）、墳丘測量調査および墳丘裾発掘調査（京都大学文学部考古学研究室）{{Sfn|近江町山津照神社古墳の調査|1995}}。 * [[1996年]]（平成8年）5月31日、県指定以外の出土品が近江町指定有形文化財（のち米原市指定有形文化財）に指定。 * [[2006年]]（平成18年）3月17日、既県指定以外の出土品が滋賀県指定有形文化財に追加指定。 == 文化財 == === 滋賀県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 山津照神社古墳出土品 一括（考古資料） **: 明細は後出。所有者は山津照神社。1961年（昭和36年）4月1日から[[滋賀県立琵琶湖博物館]]保管、2001年（平成13年）12月25日から[[滋賀県立安土城考古博物館]]保管。 **: 1957年（昭和32年）8月26日指定、2006年（平成18年）3月17日追加指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.maibara.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000003/3017/data.pdf 米原市地域防災計画 資料編]}}（米原市ホームページ）。[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaisenshu/20060317kenshitei/yamatuterujinjyakohun/index.html 県指定有形文化財考古資料の部（追加指定）]（滋賀県ホームページ）。。 * 史跡 ** 山津照神社古墳 - 1969年（昭和44年）9月12日指定[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/jourei/reiki_int/reiki_honbun/k001RG00001439.html 昭和44年9月12日滋賀県教育委員会告示第2号]（滋賀県ホームページ）。。 {{-}} {{hidden begin |title = 滋賀県指定有形文化財「山津照神社古墳出土品」の明細 |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} '''昭和32年8月26日指定物件の明細'''[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/jourei/reiki_int/reiki_honbun/k001RG00001375.html {{small|滋賀県立安土城考古博物館展示。}} == 関連施設 == * [[滋賀県立安土城考古博物館]]（[[近江八幡市]]安土町下豊浦） - 山津照神社古墳出土品を保管・展示。 * 米原市近江はにわ館（米原市顔戸） - 山津照神社古墳の再現模型を展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（滋賀県教育委員会、1992設置） * 史跡説明板（米原市教育委員会、2008年度設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=近江町山津照神社古墳の調査 -近江町能登瀬 山津照神社古墳-|title={{PDFlink|[http://shiga-bunkazai.jp/download/dayori/210.pdf 滋賀文化財だより No.210]}}|publisher=財団法人滋賀県文化財保護協会|isbn=|page=3|ref={{Harvid|近江町山津照神社古墳の調査|1995}}}} ** {{PDFlink|[http://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3063.pdf 「山津照神社古墳」]}} 滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課、2004年（滋賀県総合教育センター「滋賀県文化財学習シート」）。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=丸山竜平|year=1989|chapter=山津照神社古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|山津照神社古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=山津照神社古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 25 滋賀県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490255|ref={{Harvid|山津照神社古墳（平凡社）|1991}}}} * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=水谷千秋|authorlink=水谷千秋|year=2013|chapter=|title=継体天皇と朝鮮半島の謎（文春新書925）|publisher=[[文藝春秋]]|isbn=978-4166609253|ref={{Harvid|水谷千秋|2013}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=京都大学文学部考古学研究室|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=琵琶湖周辺の6世紀を探る|publisher=京都大学文学部考古学研究室|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} * [[山津照神社]] * [[息長氏]] {{デフォルトソート:やまつてるしんしやこふん}} [[Category:滋賀県の古墳]] [[Category:米原市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:滋賀県指定史跡]] [[Category:息長氏|墓やまつてるしんしやこふん]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[山津照神社古墳]] to [[Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 山津照神社古墳 |画像 = [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = 能登瀬古墳{{Sfn|山津照神社古墳（古墳）|1989}} |所属 = 息長古墳群 |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[米原市]]能登瀬598（字オホギ）（[[山津照神社]]境内） |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 19|緯度秒 = 47.98 |経度度 =136|経度分 = 19|経度秒 = 19.59 |ISO = JP-25 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長46.2m |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]]（内部に[[家形石棺]]） |被葬者 = （一説）[[息長氏]]首長 |出土品 = 副葬品多数・[[埴輪]]片・土器片 |史跡 = 岐阜県指定史跡「山津照神社古墳」 |有形文化財 = 出土品（滋賀県指定文化財） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Shiga |ラベル = 山津照神社
古墳 |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''山津照神社古墳'''（やまつてるじんじゃこふん）は、[[滋賀県]][[米原市]]能登瀬にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。息長古墳群（おきながこふんぐん）を構成する古墳の1つ。滋賀県指定史跡に指定され、出土品は滋賀県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|墳丘（北側）}}{{small|右に前方部、左奥に後円部。}}]] 滋賀県北東部、湖北地方の横山丘陵の南端部に築造された、湖北地方では代表的な後期古墳である{{Sfn|近江町山津照神社古墳の調査|1995}}。一帯では塚の越古墳、後別当古墳、人塚山古墳、狐塚古墳などとともに息長古墳群を構成する{{Sfn|山津照神社古墳（平凡社）|1991}}。現在は[[式内社]]の[[山津照神社]]境内に位置し、[[1882年]]（[[明治]]15年）の山津照神社での工事の際に発見され、石棺・遺物が出土している。現在までに墳丘の一部が削平を受けているほか、[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）には[[京都大学]]による調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方部が発達した後期の前方後円形で、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘長は46.2メートルを測る。墳丘表面では段築・葺石は認められていないが、石見型盾形埴輪・[[円筒埴輪]]が巡らされるほか、北側くびれ部では祭祀跡と見られる遺構から[[造出]]の存在可能性が指摘される{{Sfn|近江町山津照神社古墳の調査|1995}}。周溝は認められていない{{Sfn|近江町山津照神社古墳の調査|1995}}。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、内部に[[家形石棺]]が据えられている。この石棺からは、明治の発見の際に金銅製冠・刀剣・水晶製三輪玉など多数の副葬品が出土している。 この山津照神社古墳は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]中葉頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|近江町山津照神社古墳の調査|1995}}。息長古墳群では塚の越古墳に後続する首長墓に位置づけられるとともに{{Sfn|近江町山津照神社古墳の調査|1995}}、息長古墳群ひいては[[坂田郡]]一帯では最後の前方後円墳とされる{{Sfn|水谷千秋|2013|pp=76-97}}。息長古墳群は坂田郡の豪族の[[息長氏]]（おきながうじ）の首長墓群と推測されており、本古墳もその1つとされる{{Sfn|山津照神社古墳（平凡社）|1991}}{{Sfn|水谷千秋|2013|pp=76-97}}（古くは[[神功皇后]]の父の[[息長宿禰王]]の墓とする説もあった{{Sfn|山津照神社古墳（古墳）|1989}}）。琵琶湖対岸では同時期の古墳として[[稲荷山古墳 (高島市)|鴨稲荷山古墳]]（[[高島市]]鴨）も知られ、これらの古墳を営造した息長氏・三尾氏や両氏が関係する[[継体天皇]]を巡り、当時の歴史的背景を考察する説も挙げられている{{Sfn|水谷千秋|2013|pp=76-97}}。 古墳域は[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）に滋賀県指定史跡に指定されているほか、出土品は[[1957年]]（昭和32年）に滋賀県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1882年]]（[[明治]]15年）、山津照神社での工事の際に発見、石棺・遺物出土{{Sfn|山津照神社古墳（平凡社）|1991}}。 * [[1940年]]（[[昭和]]15年）、出土遺物調査（[[京都大学]]考古学教室）{{Sfn|近江町山津照神社古墳の調査|1995}}。 * [[1957年]]（昭和32年）8月26日、出土品の大部分が滋賀県指定有形文化財に指定。 * [[1969年]]（昭和44年）9月12日、滋賀県指定史跡に指定。 * [[1994年]]度（[[平成]]6年度）、墳丘測量調査および墳丘裾発掘調査（京都大学文学部考古学研究室）{{Sfn|近江町山津照神社古墳の調査|1995}}。 * [[1996年]]（平成8年）5月31日、県指定以外の出土品が近江町指定有形文化財（のち米原市指定有形文化財）に指定。 * [[2006年]]（平成18年）3月17日、既県指定以外の出土品が滋賀県指定有形文化財に追加指定。 == 文化財 == === 滋賀県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 山津照神社古墳出土品 一括（考古資料） **: 明細は後出。所有者は山津照神社。1961年（昭和36年）4月1日から[[滋賀県立琵琶湖博物館]]保管、2001年（平成13年）12月25日から[[滋賀県立安土城考古博物館]]保管。 **: 1957年（昭和32年）8月26日指定、2006年（平成18年）3月17日追加指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.maibara.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000003/3017/data.pdf 米原市地域防災計画 資料編]}}（米原市ホームページ）。[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/edu/katei/bunkazai/bunkazaisenshu/20060317kenshitei/yamatuterujinjyakohun/index.html 県指定有形文化財考古資料の部（追加指定）]（滋賀県ホームページ）。。 * 史跡 ** 山津照神社古墳 - 1969年（昭和44年）9月12日指定[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/jourei/reiki_int/reiki_honbun/k001RG00001439.html 昭和44年9月12日滋賀県教育委員会告示第2号]（滋賀県ホームページ）。。 {{-}} {{hidden begin |title = 滋賀県指定有形文化財「山津照神社古墳出土品」の明細 |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} '''昭和32年8月26日指定物件の明細'''[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/jourei/reiki_int/reiki_honbun/k001RG00001375.html 昭和32年8月26日滋賀県教育委員会告示第7号]（滋賀県ホームページ）。 * 獣紋鏡 1面 * 内行花紋鏡 1面 * 五鈴鏡 1面 * 三輪玉 5箇 * 直刀 3口 * 刀子 3口 - 内2口に鹿角装がある。 * 雲珠破片 1箇分 * 辻金具破片 2箇 * 杏葉 2箇 * 轡 1組 * 鐙 半足 * 鞍橋覆輪破片 若干 * 壺 1口 * 提瓶 1口 * 埴輪破片 若干 '''平成18年3月17日追加指定物件の明細'''[http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/jourei/reiki_int/reiki_honbun/k001RG00001417.html 平成18年3月17日滋賀県教育委員会告示第1号]（滋賀県ホームページ）。 * 金銅製冠 破片 一括 * 鉄刀・鉄剣 残欠 一括 * 壺鐙 一括 ** 吊金具（鉸具・兵庫鎖・柄先金具） 2組 ** 鳩胸金具 残欠 1点 * 須恵器蓋杯 3組 * 須恵器台付広口壺 1点 * 須恵器広口壺 2点 * 須恵器大型器台 2点 * 赤色顔料 一括 {{hidden end}} ファイル:山津照神社古墳出土 装身具類.JPG|銅鏡・三輪玉

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Maibara City|ja|米原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} precincts) |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 47.98 |coordinates = 136|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 19.59 |omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-{{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Maibara City|ja|米原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a stone coffin and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the {{ill|Sakata District|ja|坂田郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|ja|1882nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering stone coffins and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|ja|1940th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|ja|1957th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a stone coffin and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the {{ill|Sakata District|ja|坂田郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|ja|1882nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering stone coffins and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|ja|1940th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|ja|1957th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Maibara City|ja|米原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|ja|1882nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering stone coffins and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|ja|1940th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|ja|1957th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |image = [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Maibara City|ja|米原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} precincts) |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 47.98 |coordinates = 136|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 19.59 |omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-{{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Maibara City|ja|米原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a stone coffin and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the {{ill|Sakata District|ja|坂田郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|ja|1882nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering stone coffins and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|ja|1940th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|ja|1957th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |image = [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Maibara City|ja|米原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maibara City|qq=}} Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} precincts) |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 47.98 |coordinates = 136|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 19.59 |omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|qq=}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-{{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iegata-sekkan|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|qq=}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Maibara City|ja|米原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maibara City|qq=}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a stone coffin and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the {{ill|Sakata District|ja|坂田郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata District|qq=}} area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|qq=}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|ja|1882nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1882|qq=}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering stone coffins and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|ja|1940th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1940|qq=}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|ja|1957th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1957|qq=}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1969|qq=}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1994|qq=}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa Museum|qq=}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|qq=}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. File:山津照神社古墳出土 装身具類.JPG|Copper mirrors and magatama beads
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 馬具類.JPG|Horse trappings
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Sue pottery pottery
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|qq=}} - Houses and displays burial goods from the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. * Maibara City Okina Haniwa Museum - Features a reconstructed model of the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly cited in this article)}} * Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. "Exploring the 6th Century Around Lake Biwa." Kyoto University, 1995. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} * {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社古墳|version=99995252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Okina Clan burials]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:山津照神社古墳]] [[ja:山湾照神社古壇]] [[en:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]]

It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a stone coffin and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|qq=}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|ja|1882nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1882|qq=}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering stone coffins and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|ja|1940th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1940|qq=}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|ja|1957th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1957|qq=}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1969|qq=}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1994|qq=}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings.
