But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |image = [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Maibara City|ja|米原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maibara City|qq=}} Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} precincts) |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 47.98 |coordinates = 136|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 19.59 |omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|qq=}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-{{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iegata-sekkan|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|qq=}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Maibara City|ja|米原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maibara City|qq=}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a stone coffin and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the {{ill|Sakata District|ja|坂田郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata District|qq=}} area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|qq=}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|ja|1882nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1882|qq=}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering stone coffins and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|ja|1940th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1940|qq=}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|ja|1957th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1957|qq=}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1969|qq=}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1994|qq=}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa Museum|qq=}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|qq=}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. {{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|qq=}} - Houses and displays burial goods from the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. * Maibara City Okina Haniwa Museum - Features a reconstructed model of the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly cited in this article)}} * Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. "Exploring the 6th Century Around Lake Biwa." Kyoto University, 1995. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} * {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社古墳|version=99995252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:山津照神社古墳]] [[ja:山湾照神社古壇]] [[en:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]]

Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Maibara City|ja|米原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maibara City|qq=}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a stone coffin and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the {{ill|Sakata District|ja|坂田郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata District|qq=}} area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|qq=}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|ja|1882nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1882|qq=}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering stone coffins and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|ja|1940年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1940|qq=}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|ja|1957年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1957|qq=}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1969|qq=}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|ja|1994年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1994|qq=}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa Museum|qq=}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|qq=}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Maibara City|ja|米原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maibara City|qq=}} Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} precincts) |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 47.98 |coordinates = 136|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 19.59 |omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|qq=}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-{{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iegata-sekkan|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|qq=}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Maibara City|ja|米原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maibara City|qq=}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a stone coffin and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the {{ill|Sakata District|ja|坂田郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata District|qq=}} area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|qq=}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|ja|1882年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1882|qq=}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering stone coffins and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|ja|1940年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1940|qq=}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|ja|1957年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1957|qq=}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1969|qq=}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|ja|1994年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1994|qq=}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa Museum|qq=}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|qq=}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. {{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 馬具類.JPG|Horse trappings
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Sue pottery pottery
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|qq=}} - Houses and displays burial goods from the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. * Maibara City Okina Haniwa Museum - Features a reconstructed model of the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly cited in this article)}} * Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. "Exploring the 6th Century Around Lake Biwa." Kyoto University, 1995. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} * {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社古墳|version=99995252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:山津照神社古墳]] [[ja:山湾照神社古壇]] [[en:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]]

Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a stone coffin and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the [[:en:Sakata District, Shiga|Sakata District]] area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|qq=}}, father of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * [[:en:1882|1882]]: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering stone coffins and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * [[:en:1940|1940]]: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * [[:en:1957|1957]]: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * [[:en:1994|1994]]: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * [[:en:1996|1996]]: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * [[:en:2006|2006]]: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa Museum|qq=}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|qq=}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]] Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} precincts) |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 47.98 |coordinates = 136|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 19.59 |omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|qq=}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-[[:en:6th century|6th century]] |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iegata-sekkan|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|qq=}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a stone coffin and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the [[:en:Sakata District, Shiga|Sakata District]] area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|qq=}}, father of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * [[:en:1882|1882]]: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering stone coffins and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * [[:en:1940|1940]]: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * [[:en:1957|1957]]: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * [[:en:1994|1994]]: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * [[:en:1996|1996]]: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * [[:en:2006|2006]]: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa Museum|qq=}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|qq=}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. File:山津照神社古墳出土 装身具類.JPG|Copper mirrors and magatama beads
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 馬具類.JPG|Horse trappings
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Sue pottery pottery
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|qq=}} - Houses and displays burial goods from the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. * Maibara City Okina Haniwa Museum - Features a reconstructed model of the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly cited in this article)}} * Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. "Exploring the 6th Century Around Lake Biwa." Kyoto University, 1995. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} * {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社古墳|version=99995252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:山津照神社古墳]] [[ja:山湾照神社古壇]] [[en:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]] [[Category:息長氏]]

Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]] Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} precincts) |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 47.98 |coordinates = 136|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 19.59 |omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|qq=}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-[[:en:6th century|6th century]] |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iegata-sekkan|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|qq=}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a stone coffin and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the [[:en:Sakata District, Shiga|Sakata District]] area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|qq=}}, father of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * [[:en:1882|1882]]: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering stone coffins and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * [[:en:1940|1940]]: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * [[:en:1957|1957]]: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * [[:en:1994|1994]]: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * [[:en:1996|1996]]: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * [[:en:2006|2006]]: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa Museum|qq=}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|qq=}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. File:山津照神社古墳出土 装身具類.JPG|Copper mirrors and magatama beads
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 馬具類.JPG|Horse trappings
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Sue pottery pottery
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|qq=}} - Houses and displays burial goods from the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. * Maibara City Okina Haniwa Museum - Features a reconstructed model of the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly cited in this article)}} * Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. "Exploring the 6th Century Around Lake Biwa." Kyoto University, 1995. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} * {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社古墳|version=99995252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:山津照神社古墳]] [[ja:山湾照神社古壇]] [[en:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]] [[Category:息長氏]] [[Category:滋賀県の古墳]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Shiga Prefecture]]

Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]] Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} precincts) |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 47.98 |coordinates = 136|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 19.59 |omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|qq=}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-[[:en:6th century|6th century]] |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iegata-sekkan|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|qq=}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a stone coffin and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the [[:en:Sakata District, Shiga|Sakata District]] area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|qq=}}, father of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * [[:en:1882|1882]]: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering stone coffins and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * [[:en:1940|1940]]: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * [[:en:1957|1957]]: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * [[:en:1994|1994]]: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * [[:en:1996|1996]]: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * [[:en:2006|2006]]: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa Museum|qq=}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|qq=}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. {{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 馬具類.JPG|Horse trappings
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Sue pottery pottery
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|qq=}} - Houses and displays burial goods from the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. * Maibara City Okina Haniwa Museum - Features a reconstructed model of the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly cited in this article)}} * Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. "Exploring the 6th Century Around Lake Biwa." Kyoto University, 1995. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} * {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社古墳|version=99995252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:山津照神社古墳]] [[ja:山湾照神社古壇]] [[en:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]] [[Category:息長氏]] [[Category:Kofun of Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:米原市の歴史]]

Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]] Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} precincts) |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 47.98 |coordinates = 136|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 19.59 |omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|qq=}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-[[:en:6th century|6th century]] |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iegata-sekkan|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|qq=}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a stone coffin and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the [[:en:Sakata District, Shiga|Sakata District]] area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|qq=}}, father of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * [[:en:1882|1882]]: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering stone coffins and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * [[:en:1940|1940]]: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * [[:en:1957|1957]]: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * [[:en:1994|1994]]: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * [[:en:1996|1996]]: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * [[:en:2006|2006]]: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa Museum|qq=}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|qq=}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. File:山津照神社古墳出土 装身具類.JPG|Copper mirrors and magatama beads
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 馬具類.JPG|Horse trappings
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Sue pottery pottery
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|qq=}} - Houses and displays burial goods from the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. * Maibara City Okina Haniwa Museum - Features a reconstructed model of the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly cited in this article)}} * Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. "Exploring the 6th Century Around Lake Biwa." Kyoto University, 1995. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} * {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社古墳|version=99995252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:山津照神社古墳]] [[ja:山湾照神社古壇]] [[en:Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun]] [[Category:息長氏]] [[Category:Kofun of Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] {{Commons category|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}}

Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]] Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} precincts) |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 47.98 |coordinates = 136|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 19.59 |omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|qq=}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-[[:en:6th century|6th century]] |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iegata-sekkan|qq=}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|qq=}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a stone coffin and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the [[:en:Sakata District, Shiga|Sakata District]] area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|qq=}}, father of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * [[:en:1882|1882]]: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering stone coffins and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * [[:en:1940|1940]]: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * [[:en:1957|1957]]: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * [[:en:1994|1994]]: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * [[:en:1996|1996]]: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * [[:en:2006|2006]]: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa Museum|qq=}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|qq=}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. "Exploring the 6th Century Around Lake Biwa."
