Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]] Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} precincts) |coordinates = {{Coord|35.329994|N|136.322108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-[[:en:6th century|6th century]] |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iegata-sekkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a Sarcophagus and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the [[:en:Sakata District, Shiga|Sakata District]] area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, father of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * [[:en:1882|1882]]: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering Sarcophaguss and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * [[:en:1940|1940]]: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * [[:en:1957|1957]]: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * [[:en:1994|1994]]: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * [[:en:1996|1996]]: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * [[:en:2006|2006]]: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. {{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Houses and displays burial goods from the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. * Maibara City Okina Haniwa Museum - Features a reconstructed model of the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly cited in this article)}} * Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. "Exploring the 6th Century Around Lake Biwa." Kyoto University, 1995. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} * {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社古墳|version=99995252|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山津照神社古墳]] [[ja:山湾照神社古壇]] {{Commons category|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q48748893}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Kofun of Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a Sarcophagus and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the [[:en:Sakata District, Shiga|Sakata District]] area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, father of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * [[:en:1882|1882]]: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering Sarcophaguss and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * [[:en:1940|1940]]: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * [[:en:1957|1957]]: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * [[:en:1994|1994]]: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * [[:en:1996|1996]]: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * [[:en:2006|2006]]: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]] Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} precincts) |coordinates = {{Coord|35.329994|N|136.322108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-[[:en:6th century|6th century]] |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iegata-sekkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a Sarcophagus and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the [[:en:Sakata District, Shiga|Sakata District]] area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, father of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * [[:en:1882|1882]]: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering Sarcophaguss and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * [[:en:1940|1940]]: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * [[:en:1957|1957]]: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * [[:en:1994|1994]]: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * [[:en:1996|1996]]: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * [[:en:2006|2006]]: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. {{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 馬具類.JPG|Horse trappings
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Sue pottery pottery
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Houses and displays burial goods from the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. * Maibara City Okina Haniwa Museum - Features a reconstructed model of the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly cited in this article)}} * Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. "Exploring the 6th Century Around Lake Biwa." Kyoto University, 1995. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} * {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社古墳|version=99995252|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山津照神社古墳]] [[ja:山湾照神社古壇]] {{Commons category|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q48748893}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Kofun of Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara City}} Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} precincts) |coordinates = {{Coord|35.329994|N|136.322108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-{{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iegata-sekkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara City}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a Sarcophagus and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the {{ill|Sakata District, Shiga|en|Sakata District, Shiga|WD=Q7402853|lt=Sakata District}} area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering Sarcophaguss and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|en|1940|WD=Q18633|lt=1940}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|en|1957|WD=Q5311|lt=1957}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=1969}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|en|2006|WD=Q2021|lt=2006}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. {{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Houses and displays burial goods from the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. * Maibara City Okina Haniwa Museum - Features a reconstructed model of the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly cited in this article)}} * Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. "Exploring the 6th Century Around Lake Biwa." Kyoto University, 1995. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} * {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社古墳|version=99995252|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山津照神社古墳]] [[ja:山湾照神社古壇]] {{Commons category|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q48748893}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Kofun of Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

At that time, a Sarcophagus and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the {{ill|Sakata District, Shiga|en|Sakata District, Shiga|WD=Q7402853|lt=Sakata District}} area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering Sarcophaguss and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|en|1940|WD=Q18633|lt=1940}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|en|1957|WD=Q5311|lt=1957}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=1969}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|en|2006|WD=Q2021|lt=2006}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |image = [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara City}} Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} precincts) |coordinates = {{Coord|35.329994|N|136.322108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-{{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iegata-sekkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara City}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a Sarcophagus and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the {{ill|Sakata District, Shiga|en|Sakata District, Shiga|WD=Q7402853|lt=Sakata District}} area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering Sarcophaguss and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|en|1940|WD=Q18633|lt=1940}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|en|1957|WD=Q5311|lt=1957}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=1969}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|en|2006|WD=Q2021|lt=2006}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. File:山津照神社古墳出土 装身具類.JPG|Copper mirrors and magatama beads
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 馬具類.JPG|Horse trappings
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Sue pottery pottery
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Houses and displays burial goods from the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. * Maibara City Okina Haniwa Museum - Features a reconstructed model of the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly cited in this article)}} * Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. "Exploring the 6th Century Around Lake Biwa." Kyoto University, 1995. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} * {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社古墳|version=99995252|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山津照神社古墳]] [[ja:山湾照神社古壇]] {{Commons category|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q48748893}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Kofun of Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara City}} Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} precincts) |coordinates = {{Coord|35.329994|N|136.322108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-{{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iegata-sekkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara City}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a Sarcophagus and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the {{ill|Sakata District, Shiga|en|Sakata District, Shiga|WD=Q7402853|lt=Sakata District}} area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering Sarcophaguss and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|en|1940|WD=Q18633|lt=1940}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|en|1957|WD=Q5311|lt=1957}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=1969}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|en|2006|WD=Q2021|lt=2006}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. File:山津照神社古墳出土 装身具類.JPG|Copper mirrors and magatama beads
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 馬具類.JPG|Horse trappings
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Sue pottery pottery
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Houses and displays burial goods from the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. * Maibara City Okina Haniwa Museum - Features a reconstructed model of the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly cited in this article)}} * Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. "Exploring the 6th Century Around Lake Biwa." Kyoto University, 1995. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} * {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社古墳|version=99995252|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山津照神社古墳]] [[ja:山湾照神社古壇]] {{Commons category|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q48748893}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Kofun of Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]

Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara City|1=Maibara, Shiga}} Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} precincts) |coordinates = {{Coord|35.329994|N|136.322108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-{{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iegata-sekkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara City|1=Maibara, Shiga}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a Sarcophagus and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the {{ill|Sakata District, Shiga|en|Sakata District, Shiga|WD=Q7402853|lt=Sakata District|1=Sakata District, Shiga}} area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882|1=1882}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering Sarcophaguss and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|en|1940|WD=Q18633|lt=1940|1=1940}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|en|1957|WD=Q5311|lt=1957|1=1957}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=1969|1=1969}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994|1=1994}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|en|2006|WD=Q2021|lt=2006|1=2006}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara City|1=Maibara, Shiga}} Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} precincts) |coordinates = {{Coord|35.329994|N|136.322108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-{{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iegata-sekkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara City|1=Maibara, Shiga}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a Sarcophagus and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the {{ill|Sakata District, Shiga|en|Sakata District, Shiga|WD=Q7402853|lt=Sakata District|1=Sakata District, Shiga}} area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882|1=1882}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering Sarcophaguss and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|en|1940|WD=Q18633|lt=1940|1=1940}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|en|1957|WD=Q5311|lt=1957|1=1957}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=1969|1=1969}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994|1=1994}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|en|2006|WD=Q2021|lt=2006|1=2006}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. {{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 馬具類.JPG|Horse trappings
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} File:山津照神社古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Sue pottery pottery
{{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Houses and displays burial goods from the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. * Maibara City Okina Haniwa Museum - Features a reconstructed model of the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly cited in this article)}} * Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. "Exploring the 6th Century Around Lake Biwa." Kyoto University, 1995. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} * {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社古墳|version=99995252|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山津照神社古墳]] [[ja:山湾照神社古壇]] {{Commons category|Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q48748893}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Kofun of Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

Kofun (front square on the left, back round on the right) |alternate_name = Notose Kofun{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = Okina Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara City|1=Maibara, Shiga}} Notose 598 (Aza Ohogi) (within the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} precincts) |coordinates = {{Coord|35.329994|N|136.322108|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-25 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |area = 46.2m mound length |built = Mid-{{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (interior includes {{ill|Iegata-sekkan|ja|家形石棚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iegata-sekkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |Interred_individual = (Presumed) Chieftain of the {{ill|Okina Clan|ja|恩長氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okina Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |burial goods = Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|垣輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and pottery shards |Historic Site = Shiga Prefectural Historic Site: Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun |designation1 = burial goods (Shiga Prefectural Tangible designation1) |notes = |map = Japan Shiga |map_dot_label = Yamatsuteru Shrine
Kofun |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-West |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun''' (山湾照神社古壇, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun'') is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara City|1=Maibara, Shiga}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It features a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後土壇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) design and is part of the Okina Kofun Cluster. Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site, the burial goods excavated from the site are also recognized as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun (north side)}}{{small|Front square on the right, back round on the left.}]] The kofun is located at the southern edge of the Yokoyama Hills in the northeastern part of Shiga Prefecture (Hokubu Region). It is one of the representative late-period tumuli in the Hokubu Region{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. Together with other mounds such as Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun, Gobettō Kofun, Hitotsukayama Kofun, and Kitsunezuka Kofun, it types the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. Presently, it is situated within the precincts of the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山湾照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and was discovered during shrine construction work in 1882 (Meiji 15). At that time, a Sarcophagus and burial goods were unearthed. Parts of the mound have been leveled over time, and in 1994 (Heisei 6), an investigation by Kyoto University was conducted. The mound has a developed keyhole-shaped design typical of the late tumulus period, with the front square facing west. It measures 46.2 meters in length. Although no terraced construction or stone covering has been identified, "Iwami-style" shield-shaped haniwa and cylindrical haniwa have been arranged. Ritual sites in the northern constricted area suggest the possible existence of a {{ill|Tsukuri-dashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuri-dashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. No surrounding moat has been found{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. The burial facility features a yokoana-style stone chamber containing an iegata-sekkan. Excavations in the Meiji period revealed numerous grave goods, including gilt-bronze crowns, swords, and crystal magatama beads. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century during the late Kofun period{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. It is considered the successor to the Tsuka-no-Koshi Kofun as the chieftain’s tomb within the Okina Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}} and the last keyhole-shaped tumulus in the {{ill|Sakata District, Shiga|en|Sakata District, Shiga|WD=Q7402853|lt=Sakata District|1=Sakata District, Shiga}} area{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. The cluster is believed to be the burial site for chieftains of the Okina Clan, with this kofun being one of their key tombs{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}{{Sfn|Chiaki Mizutani|2013|pp=76-97}}. Historically, it was even speculated to be the tomb of {{ill|Prince Okina|ja|恩長宿禦王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okina|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. == Archaeological Timeline == * {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882|1=1882}}: Discovered during shrine construction, uncovering Sarcophaguss and burial goods{{Sfn|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun (Heibonsha)|1991}}. * {{ill|1940|en|1940|WD=Q18633|lt=1940|1=1940}}: Excavation of burial goods conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1957|en|1957|WD=Q5311|lt=1957|1=1957}}: Majority of burial goods Historic Site1 as Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (August 26). * {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=1969|1=1969}}: Designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site (September 12). * {{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994|1=1994}}: Survey and excavation of the mound conducted by Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory{{Sfn|Investigation of Okina Town Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|1995}}. * {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}}: burial goods not previously Historic Site1 were recognized as cultural properties by Okina Town (later Maibara City). * {{ill|2006|en|2006|WD=Q2021|lt=2006|1=2006}}: Additional burial goods were included in the Shiga Prefectural Historic Site listing (March 17). == Historic Site == === Shiga Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site: ** burial goods excavated from Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun *** Items include gilt-bronze crowns, iron swords, ceremonial pottery, and decorative fittings. Since 1961, most burial goods have been housed at the {{ill|Lake Biwa Museum|ja|湖濱博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with some later transferred to the {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. *** First Historic Site: August 26, 1957; additional Historic Site: March 17, 2006. * Historic Site: ** Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun - Designated September 12, 1969. {{small|Displayed at Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum.}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja|安園城考古博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi Castle Archaeology Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Houses and displays burial goods from the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. * Maibara City Okina Haniwa Museum - Features a reconstructed model of the Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly cited in this article)}} * Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory. "Exploring the 6th Century Around Lake Biwa."
